violations.text: Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. New Violation. Correct By: 03/03/2026
Establishment does not have adequate shelving and/or speed racks to facilitate proper cooling. New Violation. Correct By: 03/17/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/27/2026

Mashed potato and pasta, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. Disposed. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/27/2026

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per observation, mashed potato and pasta was placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/27/2026
The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety licensed with RIDOH. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/15/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/27/2026

The quaternary ammonium concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink auto-dispenser was 0 parts per million (ppm). According to the manufacturer's use directions, the concentration of the quaternary ammonium must be 200 ppm. Chemical to be dispensed manually. New Violation. Correct By: 02/10/2026

Potatoes, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. Disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/27/2026

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per observation, potatoes were placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. New Violation. Correct By: 01/27/2026
The drain plug to the middle bay 3-bay sink were not closing properly during inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 02/10/2026
A tea bag was observed in the hand-washing sink near the dish machine. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/05/2025

Lobster base and potatoes were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Staff disposed. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/05/2025
Bolognaise was observed with date mark exceeding 7 days. Staff disposed. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/18/2025
The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/05/2025

Dishes were observed in the hand-washing sink near the dish machine; a rag was observed in the hand washing sink in the rear of the kitchen, chemical spray bottles were observed hanging on the guard of a hand wash sink at the bar.. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. PIC informed. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/05/2025

Raw animal foods (eggs, beef) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (oysters, produce) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Staff relocated items during inspection, manager informed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/05/2025

Chicken soup, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. Disposed, manager informed. New Violation. Correct By: 06/05/2025
Soups were observed with dates exceeding 7 days. Disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/05/2025

Soup and bolognaise were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Staff informed inspector the items were prepared day prior, manager informed. New Violation. Correct By: 06/05/2025

Working containers of liquid ingredients were observed not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/05/2025

The presence of flies is not controlled as evidenced by the presence of small flies around the pre-flush sink at the dish machine. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/05/2025
Salad prep #1 was observed in poor repair, lid hinges observed broken. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/05/2025

The floor under the beverage station cooler has an accumulation of brown residue. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 06/19/2025
The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. New Violation. Correct By: 06/05/2025
The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. Inspector provided RIDOH guidance. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/22/2025

An open employees' beverage container was observed stored in a food preparation area. Employees shall not drink from open beverage containers in order to prevent contamination of their hands, the container, and exposed food. New Violation. Correct By: 05/22/2025

The dishwasher failed to wash his/her hands between loading soiled and unloading clean dishes from the dishwasher. Inspector observed the dishwasher rinse hands with the pre-flush spray and wipe hands dry with a cloth towel. Food employees must clean their hands after handling soiled equipment or utensils. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 05/22/2025
Employees were observed changing gloves without washing their hands. Food employees must wash their hands before putting on gloves. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 05/22/2025
The hand-washing sinks in the bar were lacking paper towels. New Violation. Correct By: 05/22/2025
Soap was not available at the hand-washing sinks at the bar. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. New Violation. Correct By: 05/22/2025

Scrub brushes and spray bottle were observed in the hand-washing sink in the kitchen, a hose was connected to the faucet of the hand washing sink in the kitchen, spray bottles were observed on the hand wash sinks at the bar. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/16/2024

Molluscan shellfish tags must be maintained for 90 days in chronological order and the date when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. New Violation. Correct By: 05/22/2025

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. Staff changed container of sanitizer during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/22/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener, slicer, tilt kettle, ice machine, stand mixer bowls) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. New Violation. Correct By: 05/22/2025

The following foods were observed held at 46-64 degrees Fahrenheit: -eggs, 64 degrees on counter (disposed) -Tomatoes, 46 and 59 degrees on ice (placed in walk-in) -Cheese, 58 degrees on ice (placed in walk-in) -liquid eggs, 64 degrees (added more ice) -Chicken, 47 degrees (added more ice) Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/22/2025

There is insufficient refrigeration equipment/capacity to maintain a temperature of 41 degrees or below. Inspector observed unused steam wells filled with ice and hotel pans on the counter filled with ice used for cold holding. New Violation. Correct By: 06/05/2025

ROP salmon and muscles were observed thawing with packaging intact. Temperatures were under 38 degrees; staff removed product from packaging during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/22/2025

Working containers of dry and liquid ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 06/05/2025

The presence of pests is not controlled as evidenced by small flies around beverage tubing area. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 06/05/2025
Salad prep #1 was observed in poor repair. Inspector observed torn gaskets and the lid hinges broken. New Violation. Correct By: 06/05/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris:-stand mixers-refrigeration doors-underside of steam table. New Violation. Correct By: 06/05/2025

The following areas in the kitchen have an accumulation of soil residue and food debris:floors and walls rear cook line-under beverage tubing-front cookline hoods. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 06/05/2025

Hose was attached at kitchen handsink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Hose was detached at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/16/2024
Various foods were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in cooler and walk-in freezer. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/16/2024
Glass washer at bar was not pulling sanitizing chemical at proper concentration. All glassware to be cleaned in 3 bay sink at bar or in kitchen dish machine. New Violation. Correct By: 05/30/2024
Handwashing sink was observed leaking from drain pipe. Equipment to be properly adjusted. New Violation. Correct By: 05/22/2024
The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2024

Perforated pans were observed in the hand-washing sinks at the bar. Hose was attached at kitchen handsink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2024

Raw animal foods were not stored properly to prevent cross-contamination. Except when combined as ingredients, different types of raw animal foods must be separated from each other during storage by using separate equipment or arrranging each type of food in equipment so that cross contamination of one type with another is prevented. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2024

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the kitchen and bar dishwashers were (0) parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. Kitchen dishmachine was observed with the incorrect chemical for the sanitizing line. Proper sanitizer was placed and machine was primed and properly adjusted. All glassware at bar to be sanitized in the 3 bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2024

Various foods in the walk-in cooler were observed held at (44-48) degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Foods out of temperature for an unknown amount of time were voluntarily disposed of. Various foods observed over ice ranging from 54-66 degrees Fahrenheit, foods were placed into walk-in freezer to blast chill. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2024
Sauces and pomegranate seeds were observed held past 7 days. Foods were voluntarily disposed of. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2024
Condensate water from walk-in cooler condenser was observed leaking unto foods in the walk-in cooler. Foods were voluntarily disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2024
Foods were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in cooler and walk-in freezer. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2024

Walk-in cooler was observed at ranging ambient temperatures of 44-47 degrees Fahrenheit during time of inspection. Liquid leaking from walk-in cooler condensers. Equipment to be proper adjusted. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2024
Handwashing sink was observed leaking from drain pipe. Equipment to be properly adjusted. New Violation. Correct By: 05/22/2024
The ceiling in walk-in freezer (chipped paint) is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/14/2024

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was 25 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. New Violation. Correct By: 03/06/2024
Splash guard needed at ice bin and handsink at the bar. New Violation. Correct By: 03/21/2024

The following were observed in poor repair: -drain stopper at 3 bay sink -slicer (PIC informed to remove from the premises) -covering under hot well (insulation exposed) -prep unit lid (poor repair) -cutting boards (PIC informed to remove from premises) -caulking at the prep sink New Violation. Correct By: 03/21/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris: -interior and exterior of oven -front of refrigeration units -ice crusher (at base) New Violation. Correct By: 03/06/2024
Faucet by handwashing sink observed leaking. New Violation. Correct By: 03/21/2024

The following was observed with an accumulation of soil residue and food debris: -window sills by space partition -floors under all equipment (especially ranges and icemachine) The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 03/21/2024

The following were observed in poor repair: -ceiling in walk-in freezer (chipped paint) -ceiling tiles in kitchen, liquor room and by entrance to the kitchen observed in poor repair or missing (rust also observed at junctures of tile) -floor tiles by the expansion joint -floor tile grout by the range -wall tiles by washer (holes observed) The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/21/2024

Protective shielding is not provided for the lights in the walk-in freezer area. Light bulbs must be shielded, coated, or otherwise shatter-resistant in areas where there is exposed food; clean equipment, utensils, and linens; or unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. New Violation. Correct By: 03/21/2024
